The model rates Germany as slight favorite with a 35% win probability, leaving Ecuador at 33.5%—a separation of just 1.5 percentage points. This is a closely matched contest in the model's assessment, with the underdog positioned nearly level with the projected stronger side.

The draw outcome carries meaningful weight at 32%, suggesting the match is likely to be competitive and tactically constrained. The narrow spread across all three outcomes (win probabilities clustered in the low-to-mid 30s) indicates minimal predictive separation; either team's victory remains plausible, and a stalemate represents a genuine possibility rather than a tail event.

No sportsbook consensus is available yet, so a model-market comparison cannot be made at this stage. The dashboard will update that contrast once bookmaker pricing materializes.
